topic.what: Quantum gatekeeper framework controls extraction-path access via four-factor context-bound recovery requiring password, shared secret, user context, and image-bound signature
topic.how: VQC-derived gate key governs shuffled pixel traversal; dual-region architecture separates header/payload; exact statevector simulation ensures deterministic key derivation; AES-GCM authenticated encryption enforces silent failure
topic.why_matters: Shifts security from content protection to extraction-path control; payload recovery requires reconstructing correct contextual state rather than simple decryption; demonstrable on real IBM quantum hardware

characteristics.vs_baseline: Best across all baselines; exceeds CAIS (0.965) and GAN-based (0.995)
Perfect recovery vs learning-based systems tolerating errors
characteristics.context: Lossless LSB over PNG-saved stego images

characteristics.vs_baseline: Low divergence indicates stable dominant state
characteristics.context: IBM Quantum ibm_pittsburgh vs local noiseless simulator

good_for: high-security payload embedding requiring contextual verification
scenarios requiring silent failure without partial disclosure
not_for: Large-scale deployment without quantum hardware access
Classical-only security models without quantum component
requires: IBM Quantum hardware access or simulator
Minimum cover image 1024×1024 for image payloads

novelty: Context-bound extraction-path control via multi-factor reconstruction
Deterministic VQC gate key derivation preserving reproducibility
Dual-region architecture resolving nonce bootstrapping dependency
Hybrid quantum-classical execution maintaining deterministic encode/decode
Hardware validation via entropy/TVD/XEB metrics on real NISQ device
All-or-nothing recovery model with silent failure across payload types
open_problems: More expressive quantum circuits for increased traversal complexity
Extension to audio and video payload types
Formalized adversarial models for context-bound security
Long-term hardware calibration drift impact on key stability
